en: A: I forgot to bring my pen.
I can lend you.

ab: yako mangowip ila maraS ka hahowar.
en: A: I forgot to bring my chopsticks.
B: I don’t have one either, let’s go buy one.

ab: ’ita’ ta’ila tabae:iw ka kaka:at kayzaeh ay?
en: A: Let’s go buy a pen, shall we?
B: Well, I still have to buy erasers.

ab: ’ita’ ta’ila tabae:iw ka kasi’aelen kayzaeh ay?
en: A: Let’s go shopping, shall we?
B: No, I’ve already eaten at home.

ab: yako mangowip ila kinrika: ka linaSo’.
en: A: I forgot to heat up my lunchbox.
B: Take it to the heat!
A: I forgot to bring my lunchbox.

ch: B:那我們一起吃我的便當吧!
en: B: Then let’s eat my lunch box together!
A: What are you having for lunch?

ab: yako ’am somi’ael noka kakiSka:atan tinal’awan ka kasi’aelen.
ch: B:我要吃學校的營養午餐。
en: B: I want a nutritious school lunch.
A: What are you having for lunch?

ab: yako hayza maraS ka linaSo’, ’ita’ ma’onhael tasi’ael.
ch: B:我今天有帶便當，我們中午一起吃吧!
en: B: I’ve brought a lunchbox today, let’s eat together at noon!
A: Let’s go play basketball, shall we?
B: Okay! Let’s go.

ab: ’ita’ ta’ila taSembet ka mali’ kayzaeh ay?
en: A: Shall we go play baseball?
B: No, I want to study.
A: Please help me with the broom.
B: Okay, here’s a broomstick for you.
A: Can you help me with the cloth?

ab: yako mam somapoeh, torong hi ’oemaw mari’ ’iniSo’.
en: B: I was sweeping the floor and you asked ’oemaw to help you with it.
A: Are you done sweeping?
B: Yes, I swept it.
A: Why don’t you clean up?
B: I’ve finished cleaning up.
A: The floor is dirty, go clean it.
B: Okay, I’ll go get the cloth.

ab: hiza hinohaes hara:i’, Sa’ila ti:iS naehan.
ch: A:那個窗戶很髒，去擦一下。
en: A: That window is very dirty, go clean it.

ch: B:等一下，我還沒掃完這裡。
en: B: Wait a minute, I’m not done sweeping here yet.
A: Where are you going later?
B: I’m going to see my teacher.

ab: kakiSka:atan patae’aes ila, So’o SiS ka kano’?
en: A: What do you do after school?

ab: yako ’am rima’ ma:tol ki kapa’onhaelan ma’an.
en: B: I’m going to sing with my friends.

ab: kakiSka:atan patae’aes ila, siya SiS ka kano?
en: A: What’s he doing after school?
B: He’s going swimming.
